task: Write a Python function top_k(nums, k) returning a list of the k largest values in descending order, keeping duplicates. If k is zero or negative return []. If k exceeds the list length return all values sorted descending. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The function correctly handles all cases, including when k exceeds the list length.
trace: I used the `heapq` module to efficiently handle large datasets and maintain the order of values. For smaller lists, it is more practical to sort them directly using list's built-in sort method. The edge case I addressed is when the user requests more elements than the actual list length. In that scenario, I return all elements sorted in descending order.
